beats.narration: One word separates the two documents that govern employer-based certification. The recommended practice says should. CP-189 says shall.

payload.title: SHOULD VS SHALL
payload.sub: When guidance hardens into a binding standard.

beats.narration: CP-189 is a balloted American National Standard. Same employer-based world as the recommended practice, but its requirements are binding minimums, written with shall.

payload.heading: What kind of document it is

beats.narration: So tailoring changes. Under the recommended practice you modify with justification. Under the standard the written practice may add or tighten — never relax a minimum.

payload.heading: How much the employer can bend it

beats.narration: And the sharpest difference sits at the top. To certify a Level Three, the standard demands the central ASNT certificate first — then your specific exam.

payload.heading: The Level III floor under the standard
payload.note: CENTRAL ASNT CERTIFICATE → EMPLOYER EXAM → CERTIFIED

beats.narration: So check first which document the contract invokes. The recommended practice bends to fit you. The standard makes you rise to fit it.

payload.sub: That one fact governs every line of your written practice.
